Language exam preparation

Prepare for your Canadian immigration language exam.

Practice vocabulary, grammar, reading, and listening for the exams IRCC accepts for permanent residency and citizenship applications.

IELTS General Training

The world's most widely accepted English test for Canadian immigration.

CELPIP General

A Canadian-designed English test, taken entirely on computer.

PTE Core

A fully computer-scored English test built for Canadian immigration.

TEF Canada

The standard French-language test for Canadian immigration.

Which language test do you need for Canadian immigration?

IRCC only accepts specific approved tests — results convert to a CLB (English) or NCLC (French) score, which is what your Express Entry or citizenship application actually uses.

IELTS General Training

Mix of paper and computer formats. Speaking is a live interview with a human examiner.

CELPIP General

Fully computer-delivered, made in Canada. Speaking is recorded, not live.

PTE Core

Fully computer-delivered and AI-scored. Faster results, no live interview.

TEF Canada

The standard French test, especially relevant for Quebec-bound applicants and extra CRS points.

Why practice all four here

Every test scores the same 4 skills — reading, writing, listening, speaking — but vocabulary and grammar are the shared foundation underneath all of them. Practice here, then compare formats before booking your official test.
